skill.name: powershell-windows
skill.description: Guidance for writing Windows PowerShell scripts with correct syntax, safer path handling, null checks, JSON usage, and error handling patterns.
skill.summary: Helps AI assistants produce more reliable Windows PowerShell scripts.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many PowerShell and command-like snippets, but they are Markdown documentation examples rather than executable skill code. No prompt injection, malicious intent, network access, credential access, obfuscation, or automatic command execution was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: PowerShell Examples in Documentation
low_findings.description: The flagged PowerShell commands appear inside Markdown tables and fenced examples that explain correct syntax. They are not executed by the skill and do not request dangerous actions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding content is a PowerShell style guide and script template. No file contains executable automation that runs these snippets.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Signal
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography match does not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited lines are front matter and descriptive text for a PowerShell guidance skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, certificate handling, or password storage logic is present in the reviewed file. The reported line only describes the skill.

content.value_statement: Windows PowerShell has syntax rules that can cause scripts to fail in subtle ways. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise patterns for safer scripting.
actual_capabilities: Explains when PowerShell cmdlet calls need parentheses in logical expressions.
Guides assistants to avoid Unicode characters in Windows PowerShell scripts.
Provides null-check patterns for arrays, strings, and object properties.
Shows safer Windows path construction with Join-Path.
Defines JSON conversion patterns with explicit depth settings.
Supplies a basic try/catch script template with strict mode.
limitations: Does not execute, test, or validate PowerShell scripts.
Focuses on Windows PowerShell patterns, not every PowerShell Core feature.
Does not provide security hardening for privileged administration scripts.
Contains concise examples rather than full production script frameworks.

use_cases.title: Create Reliable Automation Scripts
use_cases.description: Use the syntax and template guidance when drafting Windows automation scripts that need predictable error handling.

use_cases.title: Review Generated PowerShell
use_cases.description: Check AI-generated PowerShell for common mistakes with operators, null checks, paths, and JSON depth.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Script Style
use_cases.description: Apply concise conventions for ASCII output, path handling, and structured error behavior across shared scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Simple Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Windows PowerShell script for this task. Follow the powershell-windows patterns for ASCII output, null checks, and error handling: [describe task].

prompt_templates.title: Fix Syntax Problems
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Windows PowerShell script for operator syntax, missing parentheses, null checks, and Unicode output problems: [paste script].

prompt_templates.title: Improve File and JSON Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this PowerShell workflow to use safer path construction and explicit JSON depth. Explain each change briefly: [describe workflow].

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Production Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a detailed review of this Windows PowerShell script using the powershell-windows rules. Focus on error behavior, path safety, object access, and maintainability: [paste script].

output_examples.input: Ask for help fixing a conditional expression that calls Test-Path twice.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the missing parentheses around each cmdlet call and describes the corrected conditional structure.

output_examples.input: Ask for a review of a script that writes nested objects to JSON.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends adding an explicit depth value and explains why nested PowerShell objects can otherwise be truncated.

output_examples.input: Ask for a script template for a Windows automation task.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a compact script structure with strict mode, path setup, try/catch handling, and ASCII status messages.

best_practices: Ask for PowerShell output that avoids Unicode symbols when targeting Windows consoles.
Request explicit null checks before property access on optional values.
Include expected error behavior when asking for generated scripts.
anti_patterns: Do not assume Bash-style syntax works in PowerShell.
Do not omit JSON depth when serializing nested objects.
Do not ask the assistant to run administrative commands without review.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants write and review Windows PowerShell scripts using safer syntax and common reliability patterns.

faq.question: Does the skill run PowerShell commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guidance file and does not include executable automation.

faq.question: Is this only for Windows PowerShell?
faq.answer: The guidance is focused on Windows PowerShell behavior and common Windows scripting pitfalls.

faq.question: Can it review existing scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. You can ask an assistant to apply the listed patterns to an existing script.

faq.question: Does it replace script testing?
faq.answer: No. Generated or reviewed scripts still need testing in the target Windows environment.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
